choices.text: Plan → code → execute (in sandbox) → review → loop if errors. Self-correction via feedback. Sandboxing essential (E2B, Modal, Docker). Iteration limit prevents infinite loops. Pattern used by Cursor, Aider, SWE-agent, Copilot Workspace.

questions.explanation: Plan → code → execute → review → loop. Sandbox + limits required.

questions.prompt: Code agent runs untrusted code on production server. Risks?

choices.text: Massive security risk. Code agents must NEVER run untrusted code directly on host: rm -rf /, install malware, exfiltrate data, mine crypto, etc. LLMs can hallucinate dangerous code or be prompt-injected. Required: sandboxing (isolated environment with no host access). Options: 1) E2B (managed sandbox API). 2) Modal containers. 3) Docker with limited capabilities + no network OR restricted network. 4) Cloud VMs (ephemeral, destroyed after). 5) Python subprocess with seccomp + cgroups. 6) WebContainer / Pyodide for browser-only. Never: direct subprocess.run() on production host. Pro habit: every code-executing agent has sandboxing as Day 1 requirement; not afterthought.

questions.explanation: Sandboxing essential. E2B / Modal / Docker / cloud VMs. Never direct exec on host.
